claim: Agent Kernel can connect to a live local model_stack server backed by real local LLaMA-family weights and complete a delegated queue job with provider=model_stack.

runtime_patch: serve/runtime.py now supports MODEL_STACK_HF_LLAMA_SNAPSHOT_DIR / HF_LLAMA_SNAPSHOT_DIR for direct HF LLaMA snapshot loading through build_local_llama_from_snapshot.
runtime/checkpoint.py now chooses the target device before HF weight assignment to avoid CPU full-model load pressure on this machine.
specs/config.py and runtime/checkpoint.py now preserve n_kv_heads from HF LLaMA configs so GQA cache allocation matches attention output.

related_same_weight_qwen_status.model_stack_qwen_importer: not present in checked model-stack import surface
related_same_weight_qwen_status.blocker: Same-weight Qwen model-stack inference remains open until model-stack has a Qwen3.5 config and weight mapper.

limitations: This is a real local-weight model_stack proof, but the served model is LLaMA 3.2 3B, not the Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B weights currently served by vLLM.
The delegated queue task used the deterministic git_repo_review_direct command path, not a model_stack-generated JSON decision.
A LLaMA 3.1 8B server attempt was killed during first load on this 31 GiB RAM host; LLaMA 3.2 3B is the viable local proof target for now.
The default system Python still has a broken namespace-only torch install; model-stack tests and serving used /home/peyton/miniconda3/envs/ai/bin/python.
a5_interpretation: This upgrades the model_stack provider proof from tiny-random runtime wiring to live token generation from real local LLaMA-family weights plus a successful Agent Kernel delegated queue job. It strengthens provider independence for the A4->A5 lane, while same-weight Qwen and model_stack-generated JSON decision quality remain separate open proof targets.
